name: TG Frida Mono
classification_notes: Explicitly described as a monospaced font combining geometric design with humanist-style details, placing it squarely in the geometric mono category.
summary: A geometric monospaced typeface with humanist details across six weights, designed by Iqbal Firdaus for Tegamitype.
description: Six weights from Light to ExtraBold make up TG Frida Mono, a geometric monospaced design that incorporates humanist-style stroke details to soften its technical precision. Alternate characters are included to extend design possibilities beyond the standard character set. Available in OTF, TTF, WOFF and WOFF2 formats with broad Latin script coverage spanning over 100 languages.
foundry_blurb: Tegamitype describe TG Frida Mono as a cutting-edge digital monospaced font that blends geometric design with humanist-style details, carefully crafted across six weights from Light to ExtraBold. They highlight the inclusion of alternate characters as a means of expanding design possibilities, and position the typeface as a versatile choice for projects requiring precision and regularity.

name: TG Riota Gothic
classification_notes: The foundry explicitly describes it as a geometric-style digital sans serif, with single-storey alternate forms and clean proportions consistent with geometric sans conventions.
summary: A geometric sans from Tegamitype with seven weights, matching italics and variable font support across more than 90 Latin-based languages.
description: Designed by Iqbal Firdaus and released in 2022, TG Riota Gothic covers 14 styles from Light to Black with upright and italic cuts, plus a variable font spanning two axes. Each style carries over 674 glyphs and includes three sets of alternates — single-storey, no-tail and square-dot — alongside a full suite of OpenType features including ligatures, lining figures and case-sensitive forms. Language support runs to over 90 Latin-based scripts, making it broadly usable for international publishing and interface work.
foundry_blurb: Tegamitype describe TG Riota Gothic as a brand new digital sans serif in geometric style with good proportional forms, suited to both small text and larger sizes. It ships with three alternative glyph groups that can be combined to meet varied design needs, alongside extensive OpenType features and coverage of more than 90 Latin-based languages across its 674-plus glyph set.

name: TG Pilgrima Office
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a sans serif on the product page, evolved from TG Neuramatica with a clean, modern aesthetic suggesting a geometric or neo-grotesque character suited to digital and print work.
summary: A clean modern sans in nine weights with matching italics and variable font support, evolved from the structural framework of TG Neuramatica.
description: Designed by Iqbal Firdaus at Tegamitype, TG Pilgrima Office spans 18 styles across nine weights from Thin to Black, each with a true italic, plus a variable font instance. The family includes a broad Latin character set covering over 100 languages and a full OpenType feature suite including ligatures, alternates and fractions. It is aimed at branding, editorial and digital work where consistent performance across point sizes is a priority.
foundry_blurb: Tegamitype describe TG Pilgrima Office as a new sans serif digital typeface evolved from the structural framework of TG Neuramatica, featuring a refined identity and expanded OpenType features. They highlight its reliability across small point sizes, mid-scale layouts and large display settings, and position it for branding, editorial and digital projects that require consistency and visual harmony. Files are supplied in OTF, TTF, WOFF and WOFF2 formats.

name: TG Reglic
classification_notes: The foundry describes TG Reglic as a contemporary sans serif influenced by both grotesque and geometric letterforms, placing it at the intersection of these two traditions rather than firmly in either camp.
summary: A contemporary sans serif by Iqbal Firdaus blending grotesque and geometric influences, available in four weights with italics and a variable font.
description: Released in 2021 by Tegamitype, TG Reglic draws on both grotesque and geometric letterform traditions to produce a versatile sans suited to body and display text alike. The family ships in four weights from Regular to Bold, each with a matching italic, plus a variable font file covering the full range. OpenType features include multiple ligature sets, a full suite of figure styles and four stylistic sets, with Latin-based language support across more than 100 languages.
foundry_blurb: Tegamitype describe TG Reglic as a new contemporary sans serif influenced by grotesque and geometric typeface letterforms, offering four weights with italic companions and variable font support. It includes OpenType features such as ligatures, lining figures in proportional, superior, inferior, denominator, numerator and fraction forms, stylistic sets 01 to 04, and coverage of over 100 Latin-based languages, making it suitable for both small and large text sizes.

name: TG Glifko
classification_notes: The foundry explicitly describes TG Glifko as a contemporary sans-serif grotesque, characterised by a mix of large and small apertures that give it an unusual, quirky quality distinct from neutral grotesques.
summary: A contemporary grotesque sans with mixed apertures that give it an unconventional, lively character across seven weights with matching italics.
description: Designed by Iqbal Firdaus and released in 2020, TG Glifko pairs large and small apertures within a single grotesque framework, producing a slightly irregular rhythm that distinguishes it from more neutral genre entries. Fourteen styles run from UltraLight to ExtraBold with italics throughout, and OpenType features include stylistic alternates, case-sensitive forms, fractions and superscript figures. Latin coverage extends to over 200 languages, making it viable for broad multilingual publishing and display work.
foundry_blurb: Tegamitype describe TG Glifko as a new contemporary sans-serif grotesque combining large and small apertures to create a quirky, dynamic and unusual feel. They note it performs well in display applications while remaining legible at small sizes, and highlight its seven weights, italic companions, OpenType feature set and multilingual support covering more than 200 languages.

name: TG Minagi Sans
classification_notes: Described as a neo-humanist sans serif with calligraphic and blackletter influences lending sharp details and stiffness; combines humanist proportions with modern sans letterforms.
summary: A neo-humanist sans serif by Iqbal Firdaus that draws on calligraphic and blackletter forms to give its letterforms a sharp, slightly stiff character suited to display sizes.
description: Combining modern sans proportions with details borrowed from calligraphy and blackletter, TG Minagi Sans achieves a distinctive angularity that reads well at headline and display scales without collapsing at smaller text sizes. The family spans seven weights from UltraLight to UltraBold, each with an oblique companion, and is also available as a variable font with two axes. OpenType support includes multiple figure styles, ligatures and stylistic sets, covering over 100 Latin-based languages.
foundry_blurb: Tegamitype describe TG Minagi Sans as a neo-humanist sans serif inspired by calligraphy and blackletter letterforms, which contribute sharp details and a degree of stiffness to certain parts of the design. Combined with modern sans letterforms, the result is a typeface with a forceful, distinct character that excels at large display sizes and headlines while remaining reliable at small text sizes. The family ships in 14 styles across 7 weights, includes a variable font file, and supports more than 100 Latin-based languages.

name: TG Frekuent Mono
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a geometric sans serif with a monospaced style; the foundry classifies it under Sans Serif, but the monospaced construction is the defining characteristic.
summary: A geometric monospaced typeface by Iqbal Firdaus spanning six weights plus a variable font, with script-inspired stylistic alternates adding a quirky counterpoint to its structured forms.
description: Built on geometric principles with fixed-width spacing, TG Frekuent Mono runs from UltraLight to ExtraBold with a variable font covering the full range. Stylistic alternates drawn from script letterforms introduce informal touches without breaking the overall rigidity of the design. The family is positioned for both body text and display use, where the tension between its structured geometry and the alternate characters provides visual interest.
foundry_blurb: Tegamitype describe TG Frekuent Mono as a modern geometric sans serif in a monospaced style, designed with high precision for a structured and modern feel. Stylistic alternates inspired by script typefaces give the family a quirky, differentiated character while remaining cohesive with the overall design. The foundry recommend it for both body and display text.

name: TG Praktikal
classification_notes: Described by the foundry as a modern geometric sans, inspired by geometric and gothic typefaces with a touch of monospace character design, placing it firmly in the geometric sans subcategory.
summary: A geometric sans by Iqbal Firdaus drawing on Bell Centinel, Whitney and Metric, with monospace-influenced character forms across eight weights and a variable font.
description: Designed by Iqbal Firdaus at Tegamitype, TG Praktikal is a geometric sans that blends influences from Bell Centinel, Whitney and Metric with subtle monospace traits intended to give it a distinctive character at text sizes. The family ships in eight static weights from Thin to Black plus a variable font, covering approximately 90 languages across Latin Extended scripts. Three unspecified stylistic flavours offer tonal variation within the same family.
foundry_blurb: Tegamitype describe TG Praktikal as a new modern geometric sans with eight styles and one variable font, inspired by geometric and gothic typefaces including Bell Centinel, Whitney and Metric, with a touch of monospace characters. They note it is designed to perform well in body text, and includes approximately 90 multilingual language supports alongside various OpenType features.

name: TG Haido Grotesk
classification_notes: The foundry classifies this as a grotesk sans-serif with post-modernist influence. Ink traps and neutral letterforms are characteristic of the grotesque tradition.
summary: A neutral contemporary grotesque by Iqbal Firdaus with ink traps for legibility at small sizes, available across nine weights with matching italics and a variable font.
description: Released in 2020, TG Haido Grotesk draws on post-modernist influences to produce a clean, general-purpose grotesque with broad application across modern design work. Ink traps built into the letterforms improve legibility at small sizes and are particularly suited to print. The family covers Latin and Cyrillic scripts across more than 100 languages, and includes alternates, ligatures and a variable font alongside the 18 static styles.
foundry_blurb: Tegamitype describe TG Haido Grotesk as a contemporary grotesque influenced by post-modernism, designed for versatility across all kinds of modern design. The family includes 18 styles across nine weights with italics and a variable font, plus ink traps for high legibility at small sizes, alternates, ligatures, and coverage of over 100 languages including Latin and Cyrillic scripts.
